skill.name: pr-template-generator
skill.description: Generate clear pull request descriptions with summaries, testing notes, review focus areas, and release context.
skill.summary: Creates structured pull request descriptions that help reviewers understand changes quickly.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found them inside documentation examples and fenced markdown templates. No executable script, prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Command Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The command-related matches are examples inside markdown templates, such as title formats, migration notes, rollback commands, and GitHub markdown examples. They are not executable skill code and do not create command injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious patterns appear in fenced markdown examples, not in runnable files. The repository contains only SKILL.md for this skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Network and Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The URL, WebSocket, Redis URL, and dependency references describe sample pull request content. They do not instruct the assistant to make network calls, read environment files, or transmit secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are example PR content and placeholder configuration values. No code path performs network activity or secret access.

content.value_statement: Pull request descriptions often miss context, testing details, and reviewer guidance. This skill turns code changes into structured PR text that supports faster review.
actual_capabilities: Suggests PR titles using conventional commit patterns.
Creates feature, bug fix, and refactor PR descriptions.
Adds sections for motivation, changes, testing, deployment, and rollback.
Generates reviewer focus areas and review-time estimates.
Provides checklists for documentation, testing, breaking changes, and visuals.
Includes guidance for GitHub markdown features and repository PR templates.
limitations: It needs access to accurate change context from the user or workspace.
It does not submit pull requests or change repository settings.
It cannot verify test results unless those results are provided.
Its examples should be adapted to each team's PR conventions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature PR
use_cases.description: Create a complete description for a new feature, including motivation, technical approach, testing, rollout, and reviewer notes.

use_cases.title: Document a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Explain a bug, its impact, the root cause, verification steps, and the rollback plan in a reviewer-friendly format.

use_cases.title: Summarize a Large Refactor
use_cases.description: Describe architectural changes, compatibility notes, test coverage, and risk areas for a complex refactoring pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-template-generator to write a pull request description for these changes: [describe changes]. Include summary, testing, and checklist sections.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Bug Fix PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-template-generator for a bug fix PR. Include the issue, root cause, solution, verification steps, regression testing, and related issue links.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Refactor Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-template-generator to document this refactor. Explain the before and after design, compatibility, test coverage, performance impact, and reviewer focus areas.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release-Sensitive PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pr-template-generator for a production-bound PR. Include deployment notes, migration steps, rollback plan, monitoring checks, risks, and follow-up tasks.

output_examples.input: A feature adds real-time notifications with WebSocket delivery and Redis support.
output: Title: feat: Add real-time notification system
Summary: Adds real-time user notifications with history, preferences, and offline fallback.
Reviewer focus: authentication, scaling, reconnect behavior, and notification user experience.

output_examples.input: A fix prevents discount codes from creating negative order totals.
output: Title: fix: Prevent negative checkout totals
Summary: Caps discount values at the cart subtotal and improves checkout validation.
Testing: Verified excessive, exact-match, expired, invalid, and partial discount cases.

output_examples.input: A refactor separates payment providers into dedicated modules.
output: Title: refactor: Split payment provider implementations
Summary: Moves provider-specific logic behind a shared payment interface.
Risk notes: No API changes, but reviewers should inspect provider registration and webhook behavior.

best_practices: Provide the diff, related issue, and test results before asking for the PR description.
Ask the skill to highlight reviewer focus areas for complex or risky changes.
Customize the generated checklist to match the repository's merge requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not use a generated PR description without checking it against the actual diff.
Do not omit breaking changes, migrations, or rollback steps to keep the PR short.
Do not include secrets, private URLs, or sensitive production data in PR examples.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It writes PR description content for a user to review and apply.

faq.question: Can it follow conventional commit titles?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes conventional commit title examples and scoped title patterns.

faq.question: Does it work for backend-only changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes sections for APIs, databases, deployments, testing, and monitoring.

faq.question: Can it handle user interface changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It reminds users to include screenshots, recordings, accessibility checks, and browser coverage.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide a summary, changed files, test results, issue links, deployment notes, and known risks.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only examples and no executable malicious behavior.
